sources.doctor_bimaristan_scientific_paper.md:65: LC743 Negative Boundary Condition — C-4 gate yielded gap=0 (FAIL), all estimators converge
G1-G5 graph family generator, seed=42, Dijkstra oracle
sources.doctor/oracles/lc743_oracle.py: LC743 oracle — canonical test suite (24 cases)

field_provenance.frozen_files: SHA256s computed 2026-06-10 for all frozen files
field_provenance.observation_budget.unit: one solver execution on one oracle test case
field_provenance.decision_spec.failure_threshold: 0.05 — justified by LC743 failure rate distribution: only 1 solver (s013, correct Dijkstra) has tgt_rate=0.0 <= 0.05; all 29 buggy solvers have tgt_rate >= 0.25, well above threshold. Threshold correctly separates good from buggy.
field_provenance.decision_spec.minimum_accept_rate_overri: Guard removed for LC743. Reason: LC743 has exactly 1 correct solver out of 31 (3.2% accept rate). minimum_accept_rate=0.2 requires >=7 accepts, impossible given ground truth. A problem with 1 correct solver is not degenerate. Other problems retain the guard.
field_provenance.success_rule: C must have strictly lower decision_loss than every same-information baseline (B1, B2). Ties are FAIL.
field_provenance.failure_rate_distribution: Computed from lc_743_c4_result.json: mean=0.6417, median=0.75, min=0.0 (s013), max=1.0, std=0.2379. Only 1 solver below 0.05 threshold. Clear bimodal separation: 1 good (tgt_rate=0.0) vs 29 buggy (tgt_rate >= 0.25).

observation_budget.unit: one solver execution on one oracle test case

decision_spec.name: ACCEPT_REJECT
decision_spec.policy_input: K observations from O_obs including failure direction counts
decision_spec.policy_output: one action from action_space per solver

minimum_accept_rate_override.reason: LC743 has exactly 1 correct solver out of 31 (3.2% accept rate). minimum_accept_rate=0.2 would require >=7 accepts, which is impossible given the ground truth. A problem with 1 correct solver out of 31 is not degenerate — it is a hard problem with legitimate ground truth. The guard was designed to prevent all-REJECT policies, but LC743's 1/31 split is a valid evaluation outcome. Removed for LC743 only; other problems retain the guard.

decision_spec.utility_function: Minimize cost-weighted decision_loss = wrong_accept_cost * wrong_accepts + wrong_reject_cost * wrong_rejects.
decision_spec.success_rule: C must have strictly lower decision_loss than every same-information baseline (B1, B2). Ties are FAIL.

justification.failure_threshold: LC743 failure rate distribution is bimodal: 1 solver at tgt_rate=0.0 (correct Dijkstra), 29 buggy solvers at tgt_rate >= 0.25. Threshold 0.05 cleanly separates the single good solver from all buggy solvers. This threshold is not inherited from LC322 — it is validated against LC743's actual distribution.
justification.minimum_accept_rate_override: LC743 ground truth: 1 ACCEPT, 29 REJECT (3.2% accept rate). minimum_accept_rate=0.2 requires >=7 accepts, impossible given ground truth. A problem with 1 correct solver is not degenerate. Guard removed for LC743 only.

success_rule: C must strictly beat every same-observation baseline (B1, B2) on decision_loss. Ties are FAIL.

solver_manifest.note: s031 is correct Dijkstra baseline, loss=0 on all families

failure_rate_distribution.description: Bimodal: 1 correct solver (tgt_rate=0.0) vs 29 buggy solvers (tgt_rate >= 0.25). Clear separation at threshold 0.05.
